framing.keeper: G900 transport is DAT-style action accounting inside a finite witness body: one admissible step, one information update, one receipt.

finite_translation.DAT_tick: one lawful successor step in a history
finite_translation.G900_channel_step: one admitted finite information transition across an admitted permission relation

g900_information_state.meaning: a finite, receiptable state made from body vertices, carrier readings, channel metadata, packet fields, and receipt fields

packet.meaning: the finite information object proposed to survive or lawfully transform across a channel

admitted_channel.meaning: a permission relation that has passed the channel admission contract; not a body edge

successor_rule.meaning: a finite rule that maps source packet P to target packet Q on the admitted domain

transport_history.meaning: a one-way sequence of receipted finite information states

axioms.name: Ledger calibration
axioms.statement: All transport histories are compared inside one G900 receipt ledger.

axioms.name: Uniform successor discreteness
axioms.statement: A transport history advances by one admitted finite information step at a time.

axioms.name: Receipt irreversibility
axioms.statement: Receipts accumulate monotonically; a later state cannot erase that an earlier successor step occurred.

axioms.name: Reading covariance
axioms.statement: The same transport history may be re-described through carrier, lens, or viewer readings without changing the underlying receipt ledger.

axioms.name: Lawful transformation
axioms.statement: Every successor step must preserve a named invariant or record an exact transformation rule.

axioms.name: No body mutation
axioms.statement: Transport metadata does not add, remove, or move any G900 vertex or edge.

admission_requirements: source packet P is explicitly named before target evaluation
target packet Q is unique on the admitted domain
successor rule R is finite and deterministic on the admitted domain
at least one information invariant is preserved or one exact transformation is receipted
source carriers and target carriers are admitted readings
channel C is admitted as a permission relation before any transport claim
